export_scope.description: Consumer-facing latest export generated from canonical research and data records.

files.description: Source rights, attribution, artifact-retention, public-export, and remediation policy records.
Release-boundary export of records proposed by accepted or applied candidates and not blocked by dependency checks.
Canonical finding records with maturity and provenance fields preserved.
Retained source text artifacts, normalized markdown references, hashes, section indexes, and access policy metadata.
All canonical result records across maturity states.
Result records with registry, full-text, agent-reviewed, supervisor-agent-reviewed, or accepted extraction-grade maturity.
Result records with registry-extracted structured values.
Result records that remain metadata, screening, abstract, or triage summaries.
Synthesis compatibility groups with poolability decisions, missing effect fields, and agent supervision metadata.
Generated graph view of source, study, finding, outcome, result, coverage, and synthesis-compatibility links.
Consumer coverage status with current/superseded flags and known-gap warnings.
Generated SQLite read model for indexed joins over canonical JSON records.
Versioned machine-readable contract for consumer-facing export artifacts.

notes: Use consumer-contract.json as the versioned machine-readable contract for stable artifact paths, maturity semantics, release boundaries, and required consumer checks.
Use read-model.sqlite as a generated query index only; canonical JSON records remain authoritative.
JSONL records preserve canonical record fields.
Use accepted-records.jsonl for the release-boundary view of accepted or applied candidate outputs.
Use results.extraction_grade.jsonl for structured result values.
Use results.registry_extracted.jsonl to isolate ClinicalTrials.gov posted-result extraction.
Use results.triage.jsonl only as a work queue or weak signal; it is not extraction-grade evidence.
Coverage status preserves high-priority gaps and marks superseded coverage assessments.
